IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO DEVELOP, OWN AND OPERATE LOW-INCOME HOUSING FACILITIES FOR FAMILIES, SENIORS, PEOPLE WITH SPECIAL NEEDS, AND INDIVIDUALS LACKING SIGNIFICANT RESOURCES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: THE MISSION OF MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA IS TO DIRECTLY FACILITATE THE PROVISION OF AFFORDABLE HOUSING TO FAMILIES, SENIORS, PEOPLE WITH SPECIAL NEEDS, HOMELESS AND POTENTIALLY HOMELESS INDIVIDUALS WHO WOULD OTHERWISE LACK THE FINANCIAL RESOURCES TO OBTAIN QUALITY, SAFE HOUSING. FURTHER, MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA PROVIDES A VARIETY OF FREE PROGRAMS AND SERVICES TO RESIDENTS OF THEIR AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECTS WHICH ARE DESIGNED TO IMPROVE THEIR LIVES AND THE COMMUNITIES IN WHICH THEY LIVE.
IRS990.Desc: RESIDENT SERVICES AND PROGRAMS ARE PROVIDED FREE OF CHARGE TO RESIDENTS OF LOW-INCOME MULTI-FAMILY RENTAL PROPERTIES INCLUDING FAMILIES, SENIORS, AND PEOPLE WITH SPECIAL NEEDS. THE RESIDENT SERVICES AND PROGRAMS FALL INTO FOUR PRIMARY AREAS - EDUCATION, COMMUNITY, HEALTH AND WELLNESS, AND ECONOMIC STABILITY. RESIDENT SERVICES AND PROGRAMS ARE TAILORED TO MEET THE NEEDS OF EACH LOW-INCOME HOUSING COMMUNITY, INCLUDING AFTER-SCHOOL PROGRAMS FOR CHILDREN, GED AND ENGLISH COURSES, EMPLOYMENT INITIATIVES AND HOMEOWNERSHIP SEMINARS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: HOUSING DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMS AND SERVICES INCLUDE ACQUIRING ACCEPTABLE LOCATIONS FOR EITHER NEW CONSTRUCTION OR REHABILITATION OF EXISTING PROPERTIES FOR MULTI-FAMILY RENTAL PROPERTIES OR SINGLE FAMILY HOUSING UNITS TO PROVIDE AFFORDABLE HOUSING TO LOW-INCOME RESIDENTS, OBTAINING FINANCING FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF LOW-INCOME PROPERTIES AND HOUSING UNITS, AND MANAGING THE CONSTRUCTION AND/OR REHABILITATION OF LOW-INCOME PROPERTIES AND HOUSING UNITS THROUGH OBTAINING THE CERTIFICATION OF OCCUPANCY AND PROJECT COMPLETION.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: ASSET MANAGEMENT SERVICES ARE PROVIDED TO LOW-INCOME MULTI-FAMILY RENTAL PROPERTIES AND INCLUDE PREPARING AND MANAGING ANNUAL OPERATING BUDGETS, PREPARING REPORTS FOR INVESTORS AND LENDERS, OVERSEEING THE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT SERVICES, AND ENSURING AND MONITORING COMPLIANCE TO MEET REGULATORY AND ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS OF RESIDENTS.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART III, LINE 12, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: RECOVERY OF BAD DEBTS - 2023 AMOUNT: $ 46,710. 2024 AMOUNT: $ 19,826. MISC REVENUE - 2023 AMOUNT: $ 169,388. 2024 AMOUNT: $ 110,821.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MHC, THE NATIONAL NONPROFIT, AND ITS CONSOLIDATED NONPROFIT CORPORATIONS ARE EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL AND STATE INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND COMPARABLE STATE STATUTES. ALL NONPROFIT CORPORATIONS ARE REQUIRED TO FILE TAX RETURNS WITH THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE (IRS) AND OTHER TAXING AUTHORITIES. ACCORDINGLY, THESE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS DO NOT REFLECT A PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES AND THERE ARE NO OTHER TAX POSITIONS WHICH MUST BE CONSIDERED FOR DISCLOSURE. FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024 AND 2023, THE COMPANY DID NOT IDENTIFY ANY UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT QUALIFY FOR EITHER RECOGNITION OR DISCLOSURE IN THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. INCOME TAX RETURNS FILED BY THE ENTITIES ARE SUBJECT TO EXAMINATION BY THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE FOR A PERIOD OF THREE YEARS. WHILE NO INCOME TAX RETURNS ARE CURRENTLY BEING EXAMINED BY THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE, TAX YEARS SINCE 2021 REMAIN OPEN.

FundraisingEventInformationGrp.Event1Nm: HOME IS JUST THE BEGINNING GALA

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: CAPITAL IMPROVEMENTS, RENTAL ASSISTANCE & WAGE ENHANCEMENTS

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF SAN FRANCISCO

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: COWELL FOUNDATION GRANT FOR COMMUNITY WORK

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: GIFT TO CCSF RECREATION & PARK DEPT
CAPITAL IMPROVEMENTS, RENTAL ASSISTANCE & WAGE ENHANCEMENTS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE MAJORITY OF GRANTS EXPENSE REPRESENTS CONTRIBUTIONS TO AFFILIATED ORGANIZATIONS. DONORS CONTRIBUTE TO THE ORGANIZATION FOR SPECIFIC CAPITAL PROJECTS OR OTHER RESTRICTED OPERATING AND PROGRAM ACTIVITIES, AND THE ORGANIZATION PASSES THE CONTRIBUTION TO A RELATED ENTITY IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE DONOR RESTRICTIONS.

ANNUALLY THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE WITHIN THE MERCY HOUSING, INC. BOARD OF TRUSTEES WILL REVIEW EXECUTIVE SALARIES TO ENSURE COMPETITIVENESS WITH EXTERNAL MARKETS AND FOR INTERNAL EQUITY IN RELATION TO GENERAL EMPLOYEE WAGES AND BENEFITS, INDIVIDUAL AND ORGANIZATIONAL PERFORMANCE, AND THE FINANCIAL RESOURCES OF THE ORGANIZATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 1A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD OF MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA HAS DELEGATED CERTAIN AUTHORITY TO AN EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE, INCLUDING THE AUTHORITY TO REVIEW, APPROVE, AND RECOMMEND APPROVAL BY THE MERCY HOUSING INVESTMENT COMMITTEE (COMPRISED OF EMPLOYEES OF MERCY HOUSING, INC. IN EXECUTIVE ADMINISTRATION) TO REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MERCY HOUSING, INC., A NEBRASKA NONPROFIT CORPORATION, IS THE SOLE MEMBER AND IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAX AS AN ORGANIZATION DESCRIBED IN SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OF 1986.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF MERCY HOUSING, INC., AS SOLE MEMBER OF MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA, HAS AUTHORITY OVER MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA IN VARIOUS ASPECTS OF OPERATIONS AND MANAGEMENT WHICH ARE SET FORTH IN THE RESERVED RIGHTS OF THE BYLAWS OF THE SUBSIDIARY CORPORATION. THE RESERVED RIGHTS HELD BY THE MERCY HOUSING BOARD OF TRUSTEES, MANY OF WHICH HAVE BEEN FURTHER DELEGATED TO THE PRESIDENT AND CEO OF MERCY HOUSING, INC., INCLUDE APPROVAL OF THE FOLLOWING ACTIVITIES: REVISIONS TO ARTICLES AND BYLAWS; MERGERS AND ACQUISITIONS; ESTABLISHMENT OF NEW ENTITIES; PLEDGING, MORTGAGING OR DISPOSING OF ALL OR SUBSTANTIALLY ALL ASSETS; OBLIGATIONS OF NEW OPERATING AND MORTGAGE DEBT; AND APPOINTMENT OR REMOVAL OF GOVERNING BOARD MEMBERS AND OFFICERS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7B
FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM 990 IS PRESENTED TO ALL BOARD MEMBERS AND COMMENTS AND QUESTIONS ARE ADDRESSED PRIOR TO THE FORM 990 BEING FILED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE AUDIT COMMITTEE OF MERCY HOUSING, INC. REVIEWS ANNUALLY THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURES AND DETERMINES WHETHER ANY ACTION IS REQUIRED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

SISTER AMY BAYLEY, RSM, SERVES AS AN OFFICER OF MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA AND PERFORMS SERVICES FOR MERCY HOUSING, INC. MERCY HOUSING, INC. AND THE SISTERS OF MERCY OF THE AMERICAS WEST MIDWEST HAVE ENTERED INTO A CONTRACT WHEREBY AMY BAYLEY HAS BEEN ASSIGNED TO MERCY HOUSING, INC. TO PERFORM SERVICES AND PROVIDE LEADERSHIP FOR MERCY HOUSING, INC. AND ITS SUBSIDIARIES. SISTER BAYLEY IS A MEMBER OF A RELIGIOUS COMMUNITY AND DOES NOT RECEIVE ANY PERSONAL INCOME. SISTER BAYLEY IS NOT AN EMPLOYEE OF MERCY HOUSING, INC. OR MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA. MERCY HOUSING, INC MAKES PAYMENTS DIRECTLY TO THE SISTERS OF MERCY OF THE AMERICAS WEST MIDWEST FOR MONTHLY STIPEND PAYMENTS AND BENEFITS RELATING TO THE SERVICES PERFORMED BY SISTER BAYLEY. THE SISTERS OF MERCY OF THE AMERICAS WEST MIDWEST ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR PROVIDING THE LIVING EXPENSES OF SISTER BAYLEY. FOR 2024 MERCY HOUSING, INC. PAID $118,445 FOR THE ANNUAL STIPEND FEE AND BENEFITS EQUIVALENT.

OTHER PROFESSIONAL FEES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 181,089. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 282. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 181,371. CONTRACT LABOR: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 498,522. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 498,522. MANAGEMENT SERVICES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 17,961,035. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 1,087,605. TOTAL EXPENSES 19,048,640.
